agency: New York City Economic Development Corporation (EDC)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting). edc.nyc and nycedc.com both sit behind a Cloudflare bot challenge (HTTP 403 'cf-mitigated: challenge') so the HTML is not directly fetchable; the site is fingerprinted as Drupal from robots.txt (/core/, /profiles/ paths) and the Cloudflare edge from response headers. NYC Ferry's ferry.nyc resolves via Cloudflare + CloudFront. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Economic Development Corporation (EDC)' via the Socrata Discovery API — it returns only 5 assets, all pulled with column schemas. Core EDC entities (projects, real estate, solicitations) are modeled from how edc.nyc presents them; EDC publishes none of them to Open Data.
headline_findings: EDC is a public benefit corporation, not a mayoral agency — and its data footprint reflects that: only 5 NYC Open Data assets carry the 'Economic Development Corporation (EDC)' label, and they are peripheral (a marketing company map, NYC Ferry ridership, and three WiredNYC broadband tables).

EDC's actual business is invisible to machines. Its ~60M sq ft real-estate portfolio, its development/capital projects, and its solicitations (RFPs/RFEIs) — the core of what EDC does — have NO Open Data twin and NO API; they exist only as Drupal pages on edc.nyc behind a Cloudflare bot challenge.

The one genuinely operational open dataset is NYC Ferry Ridership (t5n6-gx8c); the rest are a promotional 'Mapped In NY' company map (f4yq-wry5) and the WiredNYC broadband-certification program (3 datasets).
The site itself is not machine-accessible: edc.nyc and nycedc.com return 403 with a Cloudflare challenge to non-browser clients, so even the project/asset/RFP listings can't be reliably scraped.

Net-new write surface: respond to a solicitation (RFPResponse) — register interest or submit a proposal to an EDC RFP/RFEI, which today happens only by email, at a pre-submission conference, or by manual document submission. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: Only 5 EDC-labeled datasets: Mapped In NY, NYC Ferry Ridership, and 3 WiredNYC tables. SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. EDC's only real, open, machine-readable surface — and it misses the core business entirely.
Drupal (robots.txt /core/, /profiles/) behind Cloudflare; returns 403 'cf-mitigated: challenge' to non-browser clients. Projects, real estate, and RFPs live here as pages with no API and no JSON.
Alias of edc.nyc; same Cloudflare bot challenge.
Cloudflare + CloudFront. Rider-facing schedules/booking; no documented public API under EDC. Ridership is the one operational dataset EDC publishes to Open Data.
Where formal city procurement/solicitation responses are handled; EDC solicitations are not natively exposed as an API here.

fruit.name: Development Projects
fruit.notes: EDC's waterfront, industrial, life-sciences, neighborhood, and infrastructure projects — the visible core of its mission — published only as Drupal pages. No Open Data twin, no API. Modeled entity.

fruit.name: EDC-Managed Real Estate
fruit.notes: Industrial parks, markets, piers, campuses, ground leases EDC stewards for the City. Central to EDC yet entirely unpublished as data. Modeled entity.

fruit.name: Solicitations (RFP / RFEI / RFQ / RFI)
fruit.notes: Open solicitations posted on edc.nyc with no machine-readable feed. The read half of the net-new write workflow. Modeled entity.

fruit.name: Mapped In NY Companies
fruit.notes: EDC's marketing map of notable NYC companies with hiring links. A real open dataset — but a promotional directory, not EDC operations.

fruit.name: NYC Ferry Ridership
fruit.notes: Boardings by route, stop, hour, day type. NYC Ferry is run on behalf of EDC — this is EDC's one genuinely operational open dataset.

fruit.name: WiredNYC Broadband Buildings
fruit.notes: Three parallel tables of building telecom/broadband readiness and WiredScore certification. A niche EDC program, well published for what it is.

fruit.name: Respond to a solicitation
fruit.notes: Registering interest in or submitting a proposal to an EDC RFP/RFEI — today only via email, pre-submission conference, or manual document submission. No API and no Open Data twin. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

fruit.name: NYCEDC FY25 Leases List
fruit.notes: FY2025 list of leases of City-owned land managed by EDC (from the Annual Investment Projects Report).

fruit.name: NYCEDC FY2025 Project Information Spreadsheet
fruit.notes: Active Investment Projects receiving loans/grants/tax benefits in FY2025 (443 projects).

fruit.name: NYCEDC FY2024 Project Information Spreadsheet
fruit.notes: FY2024 active Investment Projects receiving financial assistance.

fruit.name: NYCEDC FY2023 Project Information Spreadsheet
fruit.notes: FY2023 active Investment Projects receiving financial assistance.

fruit.name: NYCEDC FY2022 Project Information Spreadsheet
fruit.notes: FY2022 active Investment Projects receiving financial assistance.

meta.platform: Drupal behind Cloudflare (bot-challenged); NYC Ferry on Cloudflare + CloudFront
meta.netnew: RFPResponse — register interest in / submit a proposal to an EDC solicitation (RFP/RFEI)
meta.tagline: EDC publishes ferry rides and a company map, but keeps its real estate, projects, and RFPs off the machine-readable web entirely.
meta.headline: As a public benefit corporation EDC has almost no open data about its actual business, so the work is to surface the portfolio and open the solicitation pipeline.

scorecard_notes.platform: Drupal identified via robots.txt, but the site is bot-walled behind Cloudflare (403 challenge).
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content/site API; edc.nyc and nycedc.com return 403 to non-browser clients, no JSON.
scorecard_notes.openData: 5 datasets, all peripheral (company map, ferry ridership, WiredNYC) — some open data, none of the core.
scorecard_notes.coreData: EDC's real estate, projects, and solicitations have no Open Data and no API at all.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write surface; solicitation responses happen by email / manual document submission.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface anywhere across EDC.
scorecard_notes.ownership: EDC owns its Drupal site but publishes little; NYC Ferry ops are contracted out and procurement runs through citywide PASSPort.
